title: Use the event_loop fixture
body:
This change aligns the test suite with an explicit use of the pytest-asyncio event_loop fixture. By consuming the event_loop fixture in asynchronous tests and fixtures (notably ds_client), we ensure all async code runs on a single, well-managed event loop provided by pytest-asyncio, reducing interleaving risks and improving test stability when starting up in-process Datasette instances and performing async I/O. The PR targets fixture-level changes rather than broad API changes, and aims to be compatible with the existing async tests in tests/conftest.py and related fixtures.

Rationale:
- The test suite already uses asyncio-based tests (pytest.mark.asyncio, async def tests). Making event_loop explicit helps avoid accidental creation of separate loops and aligns with recommended pytest-asyncio usage.
- ds_client and similar fixtures currently perform asynchronous startup and requests. Explicitly consuming event_loop ensures those awaitables execute on the intended loop and improves consistency across tests.

What this PR likely changes:
- Introduce event_loop as a parameter to the ds_client fixture (and any other async fixtures that spawn tasks or rely on the loop).
- Remove or minimize ad-hoc loop creation in tests/fixtures code paths if present, so they rely on the provided event_loop.
- Optionally annotate key async tests to include event_loop as a fixture parameter where appropriate to emphasize loop reuse.

Impact and risks:
- Low risk if done carefully; mainly affects test setup and fixture scoping. Real tests shouldn’t change application logic, just how the test harness runs async code.
- Ensure compatibility with existing pytest-asyncio versions in use (avoid overwriting or conflicting with any existing event_loop fixtures).

Follow-ups (optional):
- If there are tests that spawn dedicated loops in their own helper tasks, consider migrating those to use the provided event_loop fixture explicitly.
- Run the full test suite locally or in CI to verify no interleaving or resource leaks appear with the new fixture usage.

step_plan.summary: Align the test harness with an explicit pytest-asyncio event_loop by wiring event_loop into the ds_client fixture, ensuring all async startup and I/O run on a single managed loop.

steps.operation: Modify ds_client fixture to accept event_loop and use it for async startup/requests
steps.reason: To ensure all asynchronous operations in the ds_client fixture run on the explicit pytest-asyncio event_loop, improving stability and avoiding interleaving.
steps.side_effects: Test suite will rely on pytest-asyncio's event_loop fixture; ensure no conflicting loop creation; potential minor updates to tests invoking ds_client; may require re-run of tests to confirm stability
